source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The figure on the right is made up of six unit squares. Its perimeter is $14 \mathrm{~cm}$. Squares will be added to this figure in the same way until it is made up of 2013 unit squares (zigzag: alternating bottom right and top right). How big is the perimeter of the newly created figure?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text refers to 'The figure on the right' and says squares are added 'in the same way', so the shape/growth rule must be read from the image
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image asset
multi_step: solving requires inferring the construction pattern from the figure, not just reading a value
the task then requires extending that pattern from 6 unit squares to 2013 unit squares
final answer requires computing the resulting perimeter after identifying the perimeter-change rule
question is open-response numeric, so the solver must derive rather than select
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='4028', which is easy to compare against a computed result
the geometric process can be checked step-by-step once the image pattern is identified

risk_flags: growth rule is only described as 'in the same way', so interpretation depends heavily on the image

decision_reasons: Question text, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are recoverable.
Initial scoring is strong: high image dependency, good multi-step reasoning value, and high verifiability.
Although the growth rule depends heavily on the image, the required image asset exists.
The recorded issue is a metadata path mismatch, which is a minor asset-management risk rather than a loss of core content.
